title: Services, Units, Boot State, and Logs · System Lens: Journal Query
summary: Diagnose a service that is enabled but unavailable by separating desired boot state, current process state, dependencies, configuration, and logs. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux journal query increment in a service lifecycle and journal evidence packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Query journal fields, units, boots, priorities, time ranges, cursors, and follow mode without losing causal context.
Use status, is-active, is-enabled, show, list-dependencies, and failed-unit evidence before changing service state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Predict Journal Query
steps.instruction:
theory case — lab-worker.service is enabled and active, but readiness failed after configuration revision 5. Query effective unit settings and journal events from this boot before any restart or rollback.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Journal Query contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for query journal fields, units, boots, priorities, time ranges, cursors, and follow mode without losing causal context.

content.text: lab-worker.service is enabled and active, but readiness failed after configuration revision 5. Query effective unit settings and journal events from this boot before any restart or rollback.

content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Journal Query condition decided your prediction.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Capture identity, path, process, file, and service state; predict parsing and scope; run one safe simulated observation; interpret exit status; then change one bounded layer and verify rollback. Apply that move specifically to Journal Query while producing a service lifecycle and journal evidence packet.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: lab-worker.service is enabled and active, but readiness failed after configuration revision 5. Query effective unit settings and journal events from this boot before any restart or rollback. Competency probe: Query journal fields, units, boots, priorities, time ranges, cursors, and follow mode without losing causal context. A defensible solution applies Journal Query, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a service lifecycle and journal evidence packet.

content.code:
# Worked focus: linux-journal-query — Query journal fields, units, boots, priorities, time ranges, cursors, and follow mode without losing causal context.
systemctl show lab-worker.service -p ActiveState -p SubState -p Result
journalctl -u lab-worker.service -b --since '10 minutes ago'
# Restart only after evidence identifies a reversible cause.
content.caption: Runnable safe terminal evidence example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
